title: Stable Merging Sort with Incorrect Comparison

statement.markdown:
## Stable Merging Sort with Incorrect Comparison

You are given an array of integers and you need to sort it using the Stable Merge Sort algorithm.

However, there is a subtle bug in the implementation - when comparing the two elements, it's always comparing the index of the element instead of its actual value. 

Can you find and fix this bug?
statement.outputFormat: sorted array of integers in increasing order
statement.notes:
This is a hard problem and requires careful attention to detail. 

Also, note that the comparison function in the Stable Merging Sort is incorrect and needs to be fixed.

hints.text: First hint text: Pay attention to the comparison function in the Stable Merging Sort implementation.
Second hint text: The comparison function should be comparing the actual values of the elements, not their indices.

editorial.title: Editorial title: Stable Merge Sort with Incorrect Comparison
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: The correct implementation of the Stable Merge Sort algorithm should compare the actual values of the elements, not their indices.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: We can fix the bug by changing the comparison function to compare the actual values of the elements.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: The correct implementation of the Stable Merge Sort algorithm should produce the correct sorted array.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: We can use the following example to demonstrate the correct implementation: ...
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Not comparing the actual values of the elements in the Stable Merging Sort implementation.
Mistake 2: Comparing the indices of the elements in the Stable Merging Sort implementation.
